name: thank-you-for-arguing
description: Jay Heinrichs' Thank You for Arguing — a rhetoric and persuasion toolkit covering ethos/pathos/logos, Cicero's five canons, concession, reframing, and how to win arguments by changing the terms of the debate.
useCase: Master the art of persuasion — use rhetorical techniques to win arguments, change minds, and persuade effectively.
knownRisks: Rhetoric can be used unethically — the book focuses on persuasion, which some may view as manipulation
Heinrichs' casual tone may oversimplify complex rhetorical theory
American-centric examples may not translate to all cultures
